steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content: The word "although" introduces a contradiction: Philip II's government defaulted on debt yet ran a larger surplus than fiscally "virtuous" Britain. The blank names this clash between expectation and fact.

steps.title: Substitute each choice
steps.content:
The blank needs to mean a puzzling contradiction or mismatch. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — harbinger: "an intriguing harbinger" — harbinger means an omen or sign of things to come. The passage describes a present contradiction, not a forecast. ✗
  • Choice B — exemplar: "an intriguing exemplar" — exemplar means a model or ideal example. The defaulting government is not held up as a model. ✗
  • Choice C — sanction: "an intriguing sanction" — sanction means official permission or a penalty. Neither meaning fits a financial paradox. ✗
  • Choice D — incongruity: "an intriguing incongruity" — incongruity means a lack of harmony between things that don't fit together, exactly describing the paradox of defaulting while running a surplus. ✓
D is correct.
